def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    window = params['window']
    threshold = params['threshold']
    rsi_window = params['rsi_window']
    rsi_threshold = params['rsi_threshold']
    
    # Calculate rolling support and resistance
    df['support'] = df['low'].rolling(window=window).min()
    df['resistance'] = df['high'].rolling(window=window).max()
    
    # Calculate RSI
    df['rsi'] = ta.momentum.RSIIndicator(df['close'], window=rsi_window).rsi()
    
    # Generate signals
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    
    # Long when price near support and RSI oversold
    long_condition = (df['close'] <= df['support'] * (1 + threshold)) & (df['rsi'] < rsi_threshold)
    signals[long_condition] = 1
    
    # Short when price near resistance and RSI overbought
    short_condition = (df['close'] >= df['resistance'] * (1 - threshold)) & (df['rsi'] > (100 - rsi_threshold))
    signals[short_condition] = -1
    
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: EUR/GBP exhibits mean-reverting behavior when price approaches recent support/resistance levels confirmed by RSI overbought/oversold conditions.
